confirmation.detail: inbox envelope env_00f2c430-c05e-41af-a1c0-3ac89c5883a3 read back as archived

evidence: Evidence event reported current_desktop=4, target_desktop=5, delivery_case=target_on_other_desktop, cross_desktop_policy=refuse.
Task 2 was available according to narada task peek-next --agent narada-cpy.builder, so the failed nudge was a carrier/policy gap, not a task queue gap.
proposal: Define an admitted cross-desktop delivery mode such as queued notification, operator-confirmed switch-send-restore, or foreground-last-resort with visible countdown and guaranteed restoration evidence.
The bridge should return an operator-actionable message naming target desktop and the exact safe next action, instead of only throwing policy refusal.
payload.recommendation: Treat this as operator-surface ergonomics/messaging infrastructure work; keep refusal as default until a governed cross-desktop delivery mode is admitted.
payload.summary: After CPY tasks 1 and 3 were closed, task 2 became available for narada-cpy.builder. Attempting to nudge the builder through the operator-surface bridge failed because the target window was on Windows desktop 5 while the caller was on desktop 4, and the active input-delivery policy refuses cross-desktop delivery. The refusal is safer than hidden focus stealing, but the operator workflow now stalls unless the human manually switches desktop or changes policy.
payload.title: Operator-surface nudge needs governed cross-desktop delivery path

promotion.note: Envelope archived; no target-zone mutation was performed.
